growth_records.medium: Carbon-limited minimal medium (50% general salts) + 2 mM glucose + 100 uM DMSP

up.method: HPLC-inferred; 2 mM fed, consumed to <2 uM

growth_records.cite: Wirth, Joseph S. et al. Bacterial catabolism of dimethylsulfoniopropionate (DMSP). mBio (2020). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00329-20. [PMC7157515]
growth_records.snippet: R. lacuscaerulensis consumed just over 50% of the DMSP, and the concentration remaining in the chemostat was about 48 uM. ... the DMS and methanethiol released by R. lacuscaerulensis were <1% and 20% of the DMSP, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Same chemostat (D=0.04 h^-1, mu=0.04 h^-1, td=17.33 h). DMSP consumed = inflow(10) - outflow(4.82) = 5.18 nmol/min (~52%). MeSH%=1.99/10=19.9%~20%; DMS%=0.01/10=0.1% (<1%). High intracellular DMSP accumulation (36 mM) noted for this strain. Glucose qS derived: 2 mM x 0.1 mL/min = 200 nmol/min. Table 1 rates nmol/min whole 150 mL chemostat, means days 2-4, n=6, 95% CI.

growth_records.medium: Carbon-limited minimal medium (50% general salts) + 2 mM glucose + 5 mM DMSP

up.method: HPLC-inferred; 2 mM fed, consumed to <2 uM

growth_records.cite: Wirth, Joseph S. et al. Bacterial catabolism of dimethylsulfoniopropionate (DMSP). mBio (2020). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00329-20. [PMC7157515]
growth_records.snippet: In contrast, R. lacuscaerulensis consumed only 37% and released only 5% and 2% as DMS and methanethiol, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Same chemostat (D=0.04 h^-1, mu=0.04 h^-1, td=17.33 h). DMSP consumed = inflow(500) - outflow(362.4) = 137.6 nmol/min (27.5%, abstract states ~28%; Results text states 37%). DMS%=5.9/500=1.18%; MeSH%=11.2/500=2.24% (text summarizes ~5% and 2%). Very high intracellular DMSP accumulation (503 mM) noted for this strain at high DMSP. Glucose qS derived: 2 mM x 0.1 mL/min = 200 nmol/min. Rates nmol/min whole 150 mL chemostat, means days 2-4, n=6, 95% CI.
